summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting to conduct a public hearing on zoning case ZC2021-004, involving a rezoning to a Planned Development (PD) for a storage building development near State Route 28. The board discussed site plan details including building setbacks, privacy fencing requirements, the 150-foot residential district buffer, and the 50-foot property line setback. After hearing from staff, the applicant (Eric White), and discussing potential adjustments to building placement, the board voted unanimously to approve the zoning change with conditions recommended by the Zoning Commission and Zoning Director.
decisions: Adopted the agenda for the special meeting (passed unanimously)
Approved zoning case change ZC-2021-004 with conditions as presented by the Zoning Commission and Zoning Director (passed 3-0)
action_items: Eric White (applicant) to comply with all conditions of the zoning approval including inside storage only, paved entrance/drives, 20-foot minimum between buildings, 24-hour fire department access via lockbox, retention pond controls, privacy fencing, temporary sign permits, and yearly retention pond maintenance
Applicant to work with staff on adjusting building placement to meet 50-foot rear setback requirement
key_amounts: Proposed buildings ranging from 3,000 to 7,800 square feet
35 feet current setback from property line to building (50 feet required by zoning resolution)
150-foot buffer recommended from residential district for storage buildings
24 feet minimum drive width for dual traffic around buildings
180 feet wide at front of lot near Edenton Pleasant Plain and State Route 28 intersection
notable_topics: Zoning case ZC2021-004 public hearing and rezoning to Planned Development (PD)
Storage building development site plan review near State Route 28
Building setback requirements (50-foot property line and 150-foot residential district buffer)
Privacy fence requirements and front yard restrictions
Split jurisdiction between Goshen Township (Clermont County) and Warren County
Retention pond controls and drainage to adjoining properties
Clermont County Planning Commission approved 5-0 with conditions
notes: The property straddles Clermont County (Goshen Township) and Warren County, with the entrance located on the Warren County side. The Clermont County Planning Commission had previously approved the case 5-0 with conditions. Two relief items were presented for trustee consideration regarding the 150-foot residential buffer and the 50-foot property line setback. The transcript had significant audio/transcription artifacts and repeated filler text throughout, making portions difficult to interpret.
title: Spc. BOT 12.14.21 ZC2021.004.mp4
